assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 4 lists all 4 files for inspection: (1) 'get_symbols_overview on modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs', (2) 'get_symbols_overview on modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/get.rs', (3) 'get_symbols_overview on modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/summary.rs', (4) 'get_symbols_overview on common/src/error.rs -- inspect AppError enum and .context() pattern'. All 4 of the named files are referenced.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lists exactly 6 files under 'Files to Modify' and 'Files to Create': (Modify) advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs; (Create) advisory/model/severity_summary.rs, advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs, tests/api/advisory_summary.rs. Step 9 Scope containment confirms: 'git diff --name-only would list exactly the 6 files specified in Files to Modify and Files to Create. No out-of-scope files.' No unrelated files are listed for modification.

assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Files to Create' section lists: File 4 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs' (CREATE), File 5 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs' (CREATE), File 6 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' (CREATE). assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Files to Modify' section lists: File 1 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs' (MODIFY), File 2 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs' (MODIFY), File 3 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs' (MODIFY). assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 10 commit message: 'feat(advisory): add severity aggregation endpoint for SBOM advisories' followed by body text and footer 'Implements TC-9201'. The format 'feat(advisory): ...' is valid Conventional Commits (type=feat, scope=advisory, description follows colon). TC-9201 is referenced in the footer line.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 10 states: 'With trailer: `--trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code"`'

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 5 shows: 'git checkout main / git pull / git checkout -b TC-9201'. Branch name is TC-9201. Step 10 shows 'git push -u origin TC-9201' and 'gh pr create --base main', referencing the target branch 'main'. Step 1 table also shows 'Target Branch | main'.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md 'Endpoint patterns' section (line 14): 'All handlers use Result<T, AppError> with .context("descriptive message") wrapping on fallible operations.' conventions.md 'Service patterns' section (line 26): 'Method signature: Service methods take &self as the first parameter...' conventions.md 'Model patterns' section (line 29): 'Derive macros: All model structs derive at minimum Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize' and 'Module registration: Each model file is registered in model/mod.rs via pub mod <name>'. The model/service/endpoints module structure is documented across all three subsections.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 1 table includes '| Target Branch | main |'. Step 10 uses '--base main' in the gh pr create command, confirming the target branch is 'main'.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md Step 1.5 'Verify Description Integrity': 'Simulated retrieval of issue comments via jira.get_issue_comments(TC-9201). No description digest found -- skipping integrity check. This task may have been created before digest tracking was introduced. Proceeding with implementation.' The plan checks for the digest, notes its absence with a warning message, and proceeds without blocking.
